cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Sean could have gone to the bakery. The key here is to look at all the times Sean was seen doing other things and see what times are free for him to go to the bakery. Also, we need to remember that the bakery is closed after 9pm, so whatever time he goes, it has to be before 9pm. First, let me list out all the times Sean was seen: 1. Sean woke up at 5am. Not sure if that's an activity with a time frame, but maybe just a starting point. 2. James saw Sean in class from 5am to 6am. So from 5 to 6 am, he's in school. 3. David saw him buying lunch at the deli from 6am to 8am. So 6-8 am. 4. Betty saw him at the mall buying clothes from 8am to 9am. So 8-9 am. 5. Linda saw him on a rooftop from 9am to 5pm. That's a long time, 9 am to 5 pm. 6. Tiffany saw him driving to the water park from 8pm to 9pm. So 8-9 pm. Now, the bakery is closed after 9pm, so any time after 9pm is not possible. So the possible times are up to 9pm. Let me map out Sean's schedule: - 5am - 6am: in class - 6am - 8am: deli - 8am - 9am: mall - 9am - 5pm: rooftop - 8pm - 9pm: driving to water park What times are not covered here? After he wakes up at 5am, immediately goes to class until 6am. Then from 6am to 8am at deli. Then 8-9 am at mall. Then from 9am until 5pm on the rooftop. Then presumably after 5pm until 8pm, what's he doing? The next activity is at 8pm driving to the water park. So between 5pm and 8pm, there's no mention of Sean's activities. Unless there's something else, those times are free. Also, after 9pm, the bakery is closed, so even though he was at the water park from 8-9pm, after that the bakery is closed. So the possible times he could have gone to the bakery are between 5pm and 8pm. Let me check the options: A) 8pm to 9pm – but during this time he was driving to the water park, so probably not. B) 5pm to 8pm – this is the free time between rooftop
